explicit_text: Selma, Alabama, was about twenty miles from where I was born in Snow Hill. My mother taught school there during the summertime, which was a significant part of her life. Although I grew up in a small farm town, Selma was the nearby city that connected us to education and some community activities. It was a place I associated with my mother’s work and a bit of the outside world beyond our farm.
implicit_text: The city about twenty miles from where I was born in Snow Hill was where my mother taught school during the summertime, which was a significant part of her life. Although I grew up in a small farm town, that nearby city connected us to education and some community activities. It was a place I associated with my mother’s work and a bit of the outside world beyond our farm.

explicit_text: I was born in Snow Hill, Alabama, a little farm town about twenty miles from Selma. It was a small community with not many people, mostly farmers and sharecroppers like my family. I remember trailing behind my family picking cotton, though I was often just getting in the way. We moved away when I was under three years old, so my memories of Snow Hill are limited but tied closely to my early childhood and family life.
implicit_text: I was born in a little farm town about twenty miles from the nearby city where my mother worked. It was a small community with not many people, mostly farmers and sharecroppers like my family. I remember trailing behind my family picking cotton, though I was often just getting in the way. We moved away when I was under three years old, so my memories of that place are limited but tied closely to my early childhood and family life.

explicit_text: Although I never knew much about my family’s origins, I believe that because of the French name Purifoy, my family probably came from Louisiana at some point. No one ever told me exactly where my parents were born or where our family roots were. It’s a part of my background that remains unclear, but Louisiana is likely where our ancestors lived before settling in Alabama.
implicit_text: Although I never knew much about my family’s origins, I believe that because of the French name Purifoy, my family probably came from the southern state known for its French heritage at some point. No one ever told me exactly where my parents were born or where our family roots were. It’s a part of my background that remains unclear, but that state is likely where our ancestors lived before settling in Alabama.

explicit_text: We moved to Birmingham, Alabama, when I was under three years old, so I don’t have many memories of the country life after that. Birmingham became the city where I grew up and where my mother worked for some white families nearby. It was a big change from the farm life in Snow Hill, and it shaped much of my childhood experiences and family life as we adjusted to city living.
implicit_text: We moved to a major industrial city in Alabama when I was under three years old, so I don’t have many memories of the country life after that. That city became where I grew up and where my mother worked for some white families nearby. It was a big change from the farm life in my birth town, and it shaped much of my childhood experiences and family life as we adjusted to city living.
